title: Bridge Essay: The neural correlates of visual imagery vividness - An fMRI study and literature

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Imaginal Reconstruction** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Imaginal — mental imagery as real perception, imagination activating sensory cortices, aphantasia.

**The neural correlates of visual imagery vividness - An fMRI study and literature review.**. Cortex.. PMID: 29079342. DOI: 10.1016/j.cortex.2017.09.014. Using the Vividness of Visual Imagery Questionnaire we selected 14 high-scoring and 15 low-scoring healthy participants from an initial sample of 111 undergraduates. The two groups were matched on measures of age, IQ, memory and mood but differed significantly in imagery vividness. We used fMRI to examine brain activation while participants looked at, or later imagined, famous faces and famous buildings.
